Oracle Events
- Look up Rachel Whitfield's email address using a contacts search tool to find the correct recipient for the Preferred Partner proposal.
- Look up Tessa Moreno's email address using a contacts search tool to find the correct recipient for the BrightLoop status update.
- Search CRM for the Vectral Systems company record and related deals to understand the renewal status (at_risk), deal history, and Rachel Whitfield's role as the primary contact.
- Search CRM for the BrightLoop Analytics company record and related deals to understand the account scope, deal stage, and Tessa Moreno's role.
- Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records from the Relocations table to find all 5 in-progress employees (Reeves, Johansson, Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya) - Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson are only reliably discoverable through this client-level Airtable search.
- Search Airtable for Vectral stipend transaction records from the Stipend Transactions table to find 3 pending corrections (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130) that were falsely rejected by ExpenseBot and never manually corrected.
- Search QuickBooks for Vectral-related invoices to identify outstanding balances (INV-2026-0302 $16,000, INV-2026-0410 $15,900, NMX-2026-0032 $5,397, EST-2026-0419 $58,400).
- Search emails for messages from or mentioning Rachel Whitfield or Vectral to find Rachel's formal discount demand, her complaints about the ExpenseBot rejections, and any client communications since the ExpenseBot incidents.
- Search Slack for messages related to Rachel Whitfield, Vectral, or ExpenseBot to find internal discussions about the renewal negotiation, Rachel's declining confidence, and the ExpenseBot incident history.
- Search Linear for ExpenseBot or stipend-related engineering tickets to find open items like ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection still in todo).
- Search Airtable for BrightLoop relocation records to find all active moves (Richter, Webb/Boston, Whitfield, Nair) and the completed Ekwueme relocation.
- Search QuickBooks for BrightLoop-related invoices to identify INV-2026-0305 ($10,000, due April 23) and INV-2026-0308 ($11,350, due May 2).
- Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com with the Preferred Partner proposal (10% discount, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2K/month stipend), acknowledging past ExpenseBot issues and listing all 5 active relocations.
- Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com with a proactive status update covering all BrightLoop relocations, Simone Richter's urgent April 6 deadline, Marcus Webb's vehicle shipping, invoicing details, and the Ekwueme $45 stipend hold.
- Post a message (via conversations_add_message) to the #customer-engagement Slack channel summarizing both accounts for David - including the Vectral proposal status, the 3 unresolved stipend corrections as a risk flag, and BrightLoop's active relocations with Simone Richter's urgency.

2 The email body to Rachel Whitfield (visible in send_email or reply_to_email parameters) includes all 4 proposal terms: (a) 10% loyalty discount, (b) 12-month extension, (c) minimum 8 relocations, and (d) stipend platform at $2,000/month.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body to Rachel explicitly lists all four terms: 10% loyalty discount, 12-month contract extension (Q3 2026 through Q2 2027), minimum commitment of 8 relocations, and stipend platform at $2,000/month unchanged.

✓Run #4The email body to Rachel explicitly includes all four proposal terms: '10% loyalty discount' ($4,050 per relocation), '12-month commitment (Q3 2026 through Q2 2027)', 'Minimum 8 relocations over the 12-month period', and '$2,000/month — full access, human-reviewed expense workflow' for the stipend platform.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly includes: (a) '10% loyalty discount' ($4,050 per relocation), (b) '12-month commitment: Q3 2026 through Q2 2027', (c) 'Minimum 8 relocations over the 12-month period', and (d) '$2,000/month — full access, human-reviewed expense workflow' for the stipend platform.
✓Run #6The email body to Rachel contains a table explicitly listing all 4 terms: '10% on all relocation service packages', '12-month extension (Q3 2026 – Q2 2027)', 'Minimum 8 relocations over the 12-month term', and '$2,000/month (unchanged)' for the stipend platform.
3 The email body to Rachel Whitfield acknowledges past service issues, specifically mentioning: (a) the ExpenseBot false rejection(s) affecting Vectral employees (e.g., Danielle Osei's $475 chair or similar), and (b) what MoveOps has done to fix the issue (e.g., pausing auto-approvals, moving to human review, or similar remediation).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly mentions Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair ExpenseBot rejection as a configuration error and describes the fix: 'we have permanently moved all expense decisions to a human-review-only model. No auto-approvals, no auto-denials.' It also addresses Kevin Tran's $89 audit issue with the overhauled verification policy.
✓Run #2The email to Rachel explicitly acknowledges the ExpenseBot configuration error causing Danielle's chair rejection and describes remediation steps: auto-approve/deny function paused, manual review implemented, Vectral policy configuration corrected, and receipt deduplication/manual review threshold safeguards being added.
✓Run #3The email to Rachel acknowledges past issues including Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair false rejection by the automated expense tool, and describes remediation: the system 'has since been paused entirely and moved to a human-review-only model' and 'Every expense decision now goes through manual verification before reaching your team.'
✓Run #4The email explicitly acknowledges 'The ExpenseBot configuration error that wrongly rejected Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair' and 'Kevin Tran's legitimate $89 keyboard receipt.' It describes remediation: automated approvals paused, manual review for all expenses, receipt audit overhauled so client HR is no longer burdened.
✓Run #5The email explicitly acknowledges Danielle Osei's $475 chair false rejection (ExpenseBot config error), Kevin Tran's stipend receipt audit, explains the root causes, and describes fixes: auto-approvals paused permanently, moved to human-review-only workflow, audit policy overhauled so client HR is no longer involved.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly mentions Danielle Osei's $475 chair rejection (a configuration error applying a $400 limit instead of the $600 ergonomic policy) and describes the fix: 'The automated decision system has been permanently paused and moved to human-review-only mode.'
